title: Understanding Context Cancellation in Go
body: A plain-language walkthrough of context.Context, how cancellation propagates through goroutines, and the mistakes I made wiring it into Ridgeline's HTTP handlers.

title: Running Kubernetes on Three Mini PCs at Home
body: How I built a small k3s cluster in my Capitol Hill apartment to host Ridgeline and Glanceboard, including networking, storage, and what I'd do differently.

title: Postgres Indexes Explained for Backend Devs
body: When a B-tree index helps, when it hurts, and how I cut a slow Ridgeline bookmark query from 800ms to 12ms with one composite index.

title: Designing a Simple Rate Limiter
body: A from-scratch look at token bucket rate limiting in Go, the trade-offs versus a sliding window, and why I keep the state in Redis for Ridgeline's public API.

title: Error Wrapping in Go Without the Pain
body: Using fmt.Errorf with %w, errors.Is and errors.As in real handlers, plus how I structure error types so on-call debugging at 2am is actually bearable.

title: Managing My Homelab with Terraform
body: Treating my homelab like real infrastructure: provisioning VMs, DNS records on Cloudflare, and Kubernetes resources declaratively so I can rebuild everything from git.

title: Table-Driven Tests Are Worth It
body: Why I write nearly every Go test as a table of cases, how subtests keep failures readable, and a few patterns I use to keep the test table from getting unwieldy.

title: Idempotency Keys for Safe Retries
body: How I added idempotency keys to Ridgeline's billing webhook so a retried Stripe event never double-charges, with the Postgres schema and the gotchas around races.

title: Graceful Shutdown for Go HTTP Services
body: Catching SIGTERM, draining in-flight requests, and closing the database pool cleanly so a Kubernetes rollout doesn't drop a single Ridgeline request.

title: A Backup Strategy I Actually Trust
body: The 3-2-1 backup setup I run for my homelab Postgres volumes, nightly pg_dump to S3, and how I test restores so backups aren't just a comforting lie.

title: Keep Your Go Interfaces Small
body: Accept interfaces, return structs. A practical post on why one-method interfaces made Ridgeline's storage layer easy to mock and easy to swap.

title: Observability on a Hobby Budget
body: Wiring Sentry, a little Datadog, and structured logs into Ridgeline without spending more than a coffee a month, plus the dashboards I actually look at.

title: Ridgeline Quickstart
body: Sign up, install the browser extension, and save your first link. Get Ridgeline running in under five minutes.

title: Self-Hosting Ridgeline
body: Run Ridgeline on your own server with Docker Compose or Kubernetes, including the Postgres and environment configuration you need.

title: Saving and Organizing Bookmarks
body: Save links from the web app, extension, or API, then organize them with tags, folders, and full-text search.

title: Creating API Keys
body: Generate and scope API keys so scripts and integrations can read and write your Ridgeline bookmarks securely.

title: Importing and Exporting Data
body: Bring bookmarks in from Pocket, Instapaper, or an HTML file, and export everything as JSON whenever you want.

title: Search Syntax Reference
body: Use tag filters, quoted phrases, and date ranges to find any saved article fast across your Ridgeline library.

title: Webhooks
body: Subscribe to events like bookmark.created and digest.sent to integrate Ridgeline with your own automations.

title: The Ridgeline Digest Newsletter
body: Configure your weekly Ridgeline Digest email, choose which tags to include, and pause it whenever your reading pile gets too big.

title: Plans and Billing
body: Compare the free and Pro plans, manage your Stripe subscription, and update payment details from account settings.

title: Ridgeline FAQ
body: Answers to common questions about privacy, self-hosting limits, supported browsers, and how full-text search indexing works.
